event.event_id: Unique identifier for each event, ensuring distinct tracking and management.
event.event_name: Descriptive title of the event, conveying its purpose and theme.
event.event_date: Scheduled date and time of the event, formatted in ISO 8601.
event.type: Categorization of the event, indicating its nature or focus area.
event.notes: Additional information or instructions related to the event, enhancing participant understanding.
event.location: Physical or virtual venue where the event will take place.
event.status: Current state of the event, indicating whether it is open, closed, or in planning.

major.major_id: Unique identifier for each major, ensuring distinct records in the database.
major.major_name: Name of the academic major, representing the field of study offered.
major.department: Department responsible for the major, indicating the academic unit's focus area.
major.college: College affiliation of the department, reflecting the broader academic organization.

zip_code.zip_code: Unique numeric identifier for specific geographic areas within the United States.
zip_code.type: Categorizes the nature of the zip code, such as Standard or PO Box.
zip_code.city: Name of the city associated with the zip code, indicating local urban areas.
zip_code.county: Identifies the county linked to the zip code, providing regional context.
zip_code.state: Full name of the state where the zip code is located, indicating jurisdiction.
zip_code.short_state: Abbreviated two-letter code representing the state of the zip code.

attendance.link_to_event: Unique identifier linking attendance records to specific events.
attendance.link_to_member: Unique identifier linking attendance records to individual members.

budget.budget_id: Unique identifier for each budget entry, formatted as a 17-character code.
budget.category: Type of expense, categorizing budget items like Food or Advertisement.
budget.spent: Total amount of money already spent from the budget, represented as a decimal.
budget.remaining: Amount of budget left after expenditures, can be negative if overspent.
budget.amount: Initial budgeted amount for the category, represented as a whole number.
budget.event_status: Current status of the budgeted event, indicating its progress like Planning or Closed.
budget.link_to_event: Unique identifier linking to the associated event, formatted as a 17-character code.

expense.expense_id: Unique identifier for each expense entry in the database.
expense.expense_description: Brief summary of the items or services purchased.
expense.expense_date: Date when the expense was incurred, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.
expense.cost: Monetary amount spent on the expense, represented as a decimal.
expense.approved: Indicates whether the expense has been approved, always true.
expense.link_to_member: Identifier linking the expense to a specific member.
expense.link_to_budget: Identifier linking the expense to a specific budget category.

income.income_id: Unique identifier for each income record, ensuring data integrity.
income.date_received: Date when the income was received, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.
income.amount: Monetary value of the income, represented as an integer.
income.source: Origin of the income, indicating the type of funding received.
income.notes: Additional information about the income, providing context and details.
income.link_to_member: Identifier linking the income to a specific member, maintaining relational data.

member.member_id: Unique identifier for each member, formatted as a 17-character code.
member.first_name: Member's first name, representing their personal identity.
member.last_name: Member's last name, used for formal identification and communication.
member.email: Member's email address for communication and account-related notifications.
member.position: Current role of the member within the organization, indicating their responsibilities.
member.t_shirt_size: Preferred t-shirt size of the member for events and merchandise.
member.phone: Contact phone number for the member, facilitating direct communication.
member.zip: Postal code representing the member's residential area, aiding in location identification.
member.link_to_major: Reference code linking to the member's major, providing academic context.
